Pro
Github618
82 components · Tailwind 4 + React 19

The motion toolkitfor React & Next.js

Copy-paste animated components built on Framer Motion and Tailwind. Free and open source.

Browse componentsExplore Pro

Built on Framer Motion. Distributed via shadcn.

$ bunx --bun shadcn add @kineticui/tilt-cardtilt-cardtilt-card

New

Recently launched.

Wheel Picker

New

iOS-style 3D cylinder picker with physics-based flick, touch drag, mouse drag, trackpad wheel, and keyboard navigation. Controlled and uncontrolled. Reduced-motion safe.

Hero Video Dialog

New

Thumbnail with an animated play button that opens a fullscreen video dialog. Supports YouTube / Vimeo embed URLs or direct video files. Six configurable entry animations.

Feedback Widget

New

Corner trigger that morphs open into a feedback popup with message entry and animated sending, success and retry states.

Agent Input

New

Auto-resizing chat input with send / stop states, suggestion chips, and staged file attachments — wires directly into any AI SDK.

Chat Bubble

New

Animated user and assistant message bubbles with a spring entry, typing indicator, and a composable MessageList for full conversation threads.

Streaming Text

New

Character-by-character text reveal that tracks append-only target strings — simulates live token streaming. Includes a blinking cursor and a word-level TokenStream variant.

Tool Card

New

Collapsible card for AI tool invocations — shows the tool name, status (running / done / error) with spring-animated icon transitions, and an expandable input / output body.

Bloom Menu

New

A button that morphs open into a menu and blooms iris-out from the center, the grid revealing in every direction with radially staggered items.

Wallet Card

New

Wallet overview card with an account switcher and search that morph open from their triggers, a cascading balance with a live change pill and privacy toggle, copy-address, and Send / Deposit / Swap / Buy actions.

Flickering Grid

New

Canvas grid where each cell independently and randomly updates its opacity, creating a living breathing background texture. Lightweight and customizable.

Animated Beam

New

SVG beam that connects two DOM elements with an animated traveling spot of light along a static dim track. Supports bezier curvature, reverse direction, and multiple beams.

Confetti

New

Canvas particle burst (circles, squares, ribbons) fired from any screen position. Exposes a fireConfetti() function and a ConfettiButton convenience wrapper. Respects prefers-reduced-motion.

Components

Motion primitives.

Browse all

Button

Spring-pressed Button plus StatefulButton (idle → loading → success / error) and MagneticButton.

Morphing Modal

Family-app-style modal. A single panel that morphs its height as you navigate between inner views, with blur cross-fade on content.

Animated Toast Stack

Stacked toasts with status morphs, swipe dismissal, actions and layout-aware motion.

Action Swap

CTA button and slot primitives for swapping text and icons with blur motion.

Dock

macOS-style dock with grouped actions and a gliding active pill.

Tabs

Pill, segment or underline tabs with a spring layoutId indicator.

Dynamic Island

iOS-style island pill that morphs between live activity views with bouncy shell resize and blur crossfades.

Command Palette

⌘K palette with fuzzy filter, spring-animated active row and glass surface.

Expandable Action Bar

Compact icon actions that expand into labeled controls on hover or focus with shared layout motion.

Expandable Tabs

Icon tab bar where the active tab expands to a labelled pill, with a panel above that morphs height and slides content direction-aware on switch.

Tilt Card

3D perspective tilt on hover with cursor-tracked glare.

Bottom Sheet

Vaul-inspired draggable bottom sheet with snap points, inertia and glass surface.

Switch

Toggle with a spring-driven thumb and press feedback.

Tooltip

Hover or focus tooltip with blur enter/exit and spring spawn.

Text Animation

Animated text primitives for reveal sequences, shimmer loading states and letter-cascade swaps.

Number Animation

Animated number primitives for count-up values and rolling digit tickers.

Bouncy Accordion

Single-open accordion with weighted spring layout, icon rows and reduced-motion-safe content reveals.

Range Slider

Range slider with tick dots and a bouncy vertical-bar thumb that glides between snapped steps; drag and keyboard control, reduced-motion safe.

Theme Toggle

Theme toggle button with a full-page rectangle clip-path reveal via the View Transition API.

Drawer

Side panel that slides in from the left or right with a spring, backdrop blur, body scroll lock and esc-to-close.

Multi-chain Swap

Cross-chain swap widget with chain + token selectors, morphing views, animated flip and quote.

OTP Input

One-time-code input with a gliding focus ring, digits that roll in per slot, error shake and a success check draw.

Swipeable List

Mobile-style list rows that swipe left or right to reveal contextual action buttons.

Bloom Menu

New

A button that morphs open into a menu and blooms iris-out from the center, the grid revealing in every direction with radially staggered items.

Testimonials

Loved by builders.

Alex Chen
@alexcdev

Finally a component library that doesn't feel like homework to integrate. Kinetic UI's motion primitives are exactly what I wanted — zero config, production-ready out of the box.

Sara Lim
@saralim_ui

Pasted the tilt card into my portfolio and my DMs tripled. The micro-interactions are genuinely top tier.

Jordan M
@jrdnbuilds

Dropped my custom Framer Motion setup for Kinetic UI. Killed ~400 lines from our design system and the animations are actually *better* now.

Priya R
@priyabuilds

The view transition theme toggle alone is worth the install. First time I've seen that done this cleanly in any library.

Dmitri V
@dvlpr_d

Been a Framer Motion power user for years. This is what shadcn + FM should've been from day one.

Marcus T
@mtdesigns

The marquee component alone saved me an entire afternoon. Used to write this from scratch every single project.

Yuki H
@yukihdev

Kinetic UI is the reason I stopped dreading the “add some animations” comment in PRs. Just copy, paste, done.

Chloe B
@chloebuilds

Copy-paste motion components that actually look like someone cared about them. Rare. Extremely rare.

Alex Chen
@alexcdev

Finally a component library that doesn't feel like homework to integrate. Kinetic UI's motion primitives are exactly what I wanted — zero config, production-ready out of the box.

Sara Lim
@saralim_ui

Pasted the tilt card into my portfolio and my DMs tripled. The micro-interactions are genuinely top tier.

Jordan M
@jrdnbuilds

Dropped my custom Framer Motion setup for Kinetic UI. Killed ~400 lines from our design system and the animations are actually *better* now.

Priya R
@priyabuilds

The view transition theme toggle alone is worth the install. First time I've seen that done this cleanly in any library.

Dmitri V
@dvlpr_d

Been a Framer Motion power user for years. This is what shadcn + FM should've been from day one.

Marcus T
@mtdesigns

The marquee component alone saved me an entire afternoon. Used to write this from scratch every single project.

Yuki H
@yukihdev

Kinetic UI is the reason I stopped dreading the “add some animations” comment in PRs. Just copy, paste, done.

Chloe B
@chloebuilds

Copy-paste motion components that actually look like someone cared about them. Rare. Extremely rare.

Theo K
@theo_kdev

Shipped a landing page in a day using only Kinetic UI. Client asked who did the animations. I said me. I'm a backend dev.

Rafa S
@rafaelsc

The TypeScript types on these components are unmatched. No more 'string | undefined' animation disasters at 2am.

Mia C
@miacodes

Finally found a reason to actually use view transitions in production. That theme toggle is 🔥

Ben O
@benov_dev

Used this for a client project. They thought I hired a motion designer. I did not.

Ana P
@anapdesign

The button micro-interactions are *chef's kiss*. Subtle but makes everything feel premium. My clients love it.

James L
@jlcode

Tried to rebuild the action icons from scratch to learn Framer Motion. Gave up after 2 hours and just used Kinetic UI. 10/10 no regrets.

Lena M
@lenamotion

I've used magic-ui, aceternity, framer. Kinetic UI is the only one I'd ship in production without tweaking half the source code first.

Kai N
@kainvdev

Clean docs. Working components. Animations that don't lag. A trifecta I genuinely didn't think was possible in an open-source library.

Theo K
@theo_kdev

Shipped a landing page in a day using only Kinetic UI. Client asked who did the animations. I said me. I'm a backend dev.

Rafa S
@rafaelsc

The TypeScript types on these components are unmatched. No more 'string | undefined' animation disasters at 2am.

@miacodes

Finally found a reason to actually use view transitions in production. That theme toggle is 🔥

Used this for a client project. They thought I hired a motion designer. I did not.

The button micro-interactions are *chef's kiss*. Subtle but makes everything feel premium. My clients love it.

Tried to rebuild the action icons from scratch to learn Framer Motion. Gave up after 2 hours and just used Kinetic UI. 10/10 no regrets.

Lena M

I've used magic-ui, aceternity, framer. Kinetic UI is the only one I'd ship in production without tweaking half the source code first.

Clean docs. Working components. Animations that don't lag. A trifecta I genuinely didn't think was possible in an open-source library.
